carry_over_verification.g5-ivm-1_through_g5-ivm-8: all accepted/closed at fix-pass 8a19445 — still present and uncontested at HEAD (fc3f0d1). ChangeEvent.node + edge_endpoints still widened; extract_grantee / extract_event_names / extract_created_at still in place. Verified by reading all five views.
carry_over_verification.g5-p2-ivm-1: closed at 08c7386 — confirmed stderr fallback warnings present in content_listing.rs:308-313; views 1/2/5 still silent on the identity-only path, which the pass-2 disposition accepted because production paths via transaction.rs always populate event.node. No regression.
carry_over_verification.g5-p2-ivm-2: closed at 08c7386 — cost-matching Delete accounting present in content_listing.rs:335-339 and event_handler_dispatch.rs:210-220. Delete storm now consumes budget proportional to work done.
carry_over_verification.g5-p2-ivm-3: closed at 08c7386 — crates/benten-ivm/tests/subscriber_panic_isolation.rs exists (172 lines). Full pipeline: PASS 53 tests, 4 skipped (all TODO(phase-2-ivm-replay) rebuild-equivalence deferrals, no other failures).

audit_focus_findings.view_correctness: All 5 views remain correct under the widened ChangeEvent. View 1 (capability_grants) keys under grantee CID when present, fallback to event.cid; edge path wires GRANTED_TO correctly. View 2 (event_dispatch) partitions by subscribes_to list-of-text. View 3 (content_listing) sorts by createdAt via bias_i64_to_u64 (affine u64 bias preserves total order across full i64 range). View 4 (governance_inheritance) consumes GovernedBy edges via edge_endpoints and distinguishes was_truncated / cycle_detected. View 5 (version_current) consumes NEXT_VERSION via edge_endpoints and respects Node.anchor_id when present.
audit_focus_findings.view_3_as_exit_criterion_path: Engine::resolve_list_via_view_or_backend (lib.rs:2237-2298) routes post:list through View 3 with a clean fallback: probes content_listing_<label> then content_listing, falls back to backend label-index sorted in-memory by the same createdAt when IVM is disabled or the view is empty/stale. Observable ordering matches across both paths. Exit-criterion path is sound.
audit_focus_findings.grant_reader_is_NOT_using_view_1: Critical architecture observation: Engine's BackendGrantReader (lib.rs:1905-1943) reads CapabilityGrant Nodes by direct backend.get_by_label('system:CapabilityGrant') scans, NOT through View 1. Additionally, the engine writes grants with label 'system:CapabilityGrant' (line 1197) while View 1's declared input_pattern_label is 'CapabilityGrant' (capability_grants.rs:79) — a label-namespace mismatch. Phase 1 only auto-registers a live ContentListingView instance at Engine assembly (lib.rs:1808-1820), so View 1 is never wired live in Phase 1 and the mismatch is dormant. But: the definition-Node path (create_view writes a system:IVMView Node whose input_pattern_label is the unqualified form) would leave a caller who registers View 1 live with an empty index because ChangeEvents carry labels ['system:CapabilityGrant'] and View 1's has_label('CapabilityGrant') returns false.
audit_focus_findings.cascade_deletion: Partially correct end-to-end. The delete path: RedbBackend::delete_node reads Node before delete (captures labels + node body), emits ChangeKind::Deleted with populated event.labels + event.node + empty edge_endpoints. ChangeKind::EdgeDeleted events are emitted by the backend for cascaded edges (redb_backend.rs). View 1's Deleted handler extracts grantee from pre-delete node body and surgically removes; fallback iterates every entity-set. View 4's Deleted handler does `parent.retain(|_, v| v != &event.cid); parent.remove(&event.cid)` — handles both inbound and outbound references. But: no dedicated end-to-end test that constructs a graph, deletes a node, observes the cascaded edge deletes arrive via the change stream, and asserts every view converges — TODO(R4b) per lib.rs:55 remains open.
audit_focus_findings.cross_view_consistency_multi_label_node: For a Node with labels ['Post','CapabilityGrant']: the fan-out subscriber (subscriber.rs:174-179) dispatches the same event to every registered view. Each view filters internally via has_label or labels.iter().any(|l| l == wanted). A Post-labeled ContentListingView AND a CapabilityGrant-labeled View 1 would both accept the event. Verified by reading the filter sites in all 5 views — no view short-circuits on 'first match'. But: no test exercises this scenario (subscriber_routing.rs:38-45 only tests single-label routing). Low-risk because every view's filter is independent, but a regression test would solidify the contract.
audit_focus_findings.todo_markers_specificity: 4 ignored rebuild-equivalence tests carry TODO(phase-2-ivm-replay) markers that (a) name the deferred phase, (b) cite the design reason ('rebuild() is Phase-1 clear-to-Fresh (no source-of-truth)'), (c) specify the Phase-2 mechanism ('event-log replay', 'threading an EventLog or KVBackend into the View trait'). Marker in view1_capability_grants.rs:77 is most explicit. The 4 crate-level TODOs (lib.rs:48-63) cite mini-review ids (g5-ivm-12/13/14, g5-cr-1) which provide cross-reference traceability. Markers are specific enough to satisfy the R6 brief.

findings.claim: Label-namespace mismatch between View 1's input_pattern_label ('CapabilityGrant') and the label the engine writes ('system:CapabilityGrant'). Dormant in Phase 1 because View 1 is never auto-registered live, but if a caller invokes engine.create_view('capability_grants', ...) in Phase 2 the live view would receive events whose labels=['system:CapabilityGrant'] and its has_label('CapabilityGrant') filter returns false — the view would silently observe zero grants.
findings.evidence: capability_grants.rs:79: `input_pattern_label: Some("CapabilityGrant".to_string())`; lib.rs:1197: `Node::new(vec!["system:CapabilityGrant".into()], props)`; capability_grants.rs:200: `if !event.has_label("CapabilityGrant") { return Ok(()); }`. Same mismatch for the edge-path label GRANTED_TO (capability_grants.rs:174) which is unqualified while engine edge labels are convention-dependent.
findings.fix: Pick one namespace. Recommend upgrading View 1's constants to 'system:CapabilityGrant' to match the engine-write convention, OR adding a namespace-stripping helper in ChangeEvent (`has_unqualified_label`). Either is cheap. Non-blocking for Phase 1 because create_view doesn't auto-instantiate View 1.

findings.claim: The read path in View 2 unions the queried event bucket with the GLOBAL_BUCKET (empty-string key). Semantically correct for back-compat with identity-only events, but the union means a CID registered under the global bucket appears in EVERY event-name query. If a production handler ever lands in the global bucket (via identity-only path or via the edge-path which always routes to GLOBAL per lines 152-177), every event dispatch query returns it regardless of subscription.
findings.evidence: event_handler_dispatch.rs:244-263 for the trait read; event_handler_dispatch.rs:123-137 for read_handlers_for_event. Comment at the trait-read site notes 'event-name bucket with the GLOBAL_BUCKET for back-compat'. The edge-path (line 163-166) puts EVERY SubscribesTo edge event into the GLOBAL_BUCKET because edges don't carry payload — so any production path that uses the edge shape would over-dispatch.
findings.fix: Drop the edge-path GLOBAL_BUCKET default in Phase 2 when SubscribesTo edges carry event_name as a property. For Phase 1, add a test that explicitly covers 'handler subscribed to event A does NOT appear in event B query' with Node-bearing events (not the edge path) and document that the edge path is identity-only until Phase 2. Non-blocking.

findings.claim: No test exercises a multi-labeled Node routing to multiple distinct views. The R6 audit-focus question 'does an event emitted for a Node with labels ["Post", "CapabilityGrant"] route to View 1 AND View 3' is answered YES by code inspection but has no regression test.
findings.evidence: subscriber_routing.rs:38-53 tests only single-label routing. All 5 view filter sites use independent has_label / iter().any checks, so a multi-labeled event would reach all matching views — but the property is untested.
findings.fix: Add test: register ContentListingView('Post') AND CapabilityGrantsView (once its label convention is fixed per r6-ivm-1), feed a single ChangeEvent with labels=['Post','system:CapabilityGrant'] and node populated for both views, assert route_change_event returns updated=2 and each view's internal state reflects the event. ~30 lines. Fits R4b.

findings.claim: The criterion bench referenced by the R6 mandate (targets from RESULTS.md §1: Views 1,2 < 10us read; Views 3,4,5 < 100us read; < 1ms write+IVM-update) is still a todo!() stub. No empirical confirmation that the Rust implementation beats or matches the TypeScript prototype numbers. TODO(R4b) at lib.rs:48-50 names this as follow-up work.
findings.evidence: benches/view_maintenance.rs:53-58: `read_content_view_stub ... todo!("I5 — View 3 ... bench will pass once benten-ivm exposes its read surface.")`. The real read surface landed in G5 (ContentListingView::read_page, etc.) but the bench was never updated.
findings.fix: Wire the bench to ContentListingView::read_page (hot path after pre-seeding N=1000 entries), Subscriber::route_change_event (single event through 5 registered views), and one bench per RESULTS.md pattern. The Rust implementation should beat the TS prototype's 0.04us read / 0.03ms write on View 3 by an order of magnitude; the bench calcifies that claim. Non-blocking for Phase 1 exit criteria but blocks the 'meets or beats TS prototype' success metric in the R6 brief.

findings.claim: create_view() only auto-instantiates a live view instance for the content_listing family. Canonical ids ('capability_grants', 'event_dispatch', 'governance_inheritance', 'version_current') write the definition Node but don't register a live subscriber. This is a deliberate Phase-1 scope decision documented at lib.rs:1260-1265, but it means read_view('capability_grants') will hit the 'view not registered' fall-through in the subscriber and return a NotFound-style outcome even though the definition Node exists in the graph. A caller browsing the registered view definitions and attempting to read one would be confused.
findings.evidence: lib.rs:1242-1268: def is always stored via privileged_put_node; live registration only fires under `if let Some(label) = input_pattern_label.as_deref()` which is only non-None for content_listing. lib.rs:1253-1254: `already_registered` check uses subscriber.view_ids() which only contains live instances.
findings.fix: Either (a) document in create_view rustdoc that Phase 1 only auto-instantiates content_listing-family live views and other ids are definition-only, or (b) extend create_view to construct the other 4 view types with default constructors (they all implement `::new()`) when the view_id matches a canonical id. (b) is ~20 lines and closes the definition/instance gap. R4b or early Phase 2.

notes: Domain-swap audit confirms the Phase-1 IVM system is correct and converges to the algorithmic intent of the benchmark prototype: Algorithm B semantics for views 1, 3, 5 (O(1) reads after O(log n) inserts on sorted maps), hybrid A+B for view 2 (hashmap with incremental insert + per-bucket rebuild on delete), and bounded tree-walk for view 4. No blocking findings. All 5 minor findings are non-blocking hygiene / scope-boundary items that the existing TODO(R4b) queue already covers in spirit. The 4 rebuild-equivalence tests remain correctly deferred with specific Phase-2 markers. Prior G5 + G5-pass-2 dispositions (g5-ivm-1..8, g5-p2-ivm-1..3) all verified still-resolved at HEAD fc3f0d1. Recommend pass.
